items.title: June 25, 1999: Latrell Sprewell finishes the Knicks fast break with an and-1 slam during Game 5 of the NBA Finals against the Spurs.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 25, 1999: Latrell Sprewell finishes the Knicks fast break with an and-1 slam during Game 5 of the NBA Finals against the Spurs.

Sprewell finished with 35 PTS/10 REB. San Antonio won at New York, 78-77, to clinch their first NBA Championship.

items.title: “As strong a love affair as one player can have with a fan base”
items.author: Ryan Eichten
items.body_excerpt:
“As strong a love affair as one player can have with a fan base”

That line from Grady is how I will always remember Naz Reid’s time in Minnesota. From the towels to the tattoos, the connection between Naz and Timberwolves fans was special

items.title: Blazers owner Tom Dundon said he will contribute nothing to the proposed $600+ million renovation of Moda Center. Instead, he wants Oregon taxpayers to pay 100%:
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Blazers owner Tom Dundon said he will contribute nothing to the proposed $600+ million renovation of Moda Center. Instead, he wants Oregon taxpayers to pay 100%:

“It feels like we’re making a pretty big investment by staying here and paying these tax rates.”

#NBA
